quote: I'm not great at the advice. Can I interest you in a sarcastic comment? Some cheese?
note: offers humor instead of emotional support

quote: We swallow our feelings, even if it means we're unhappy forever. Sound good?
note: dark truth wrapped in rhetorical humor

quote: Oh, that makes me feel so warm in my hollow, tin chest.
note: self-deprecating metaphor for emotional emptiness

quote: I say more dumb things before 9 a.m. than most people say all day.
note: self-deprecation as preemptive shield

quote: When my parents got divorced they sent me to this shrink. And she told me that all kids whose parents get divorced have trouble with relationships. Great. Thanks for that.
note: painful story delivered with sarcastic commentary

quote: I hate Thanksgiving. It was the holiday when my parents told me they were getting divorced. My dad was sleeping with the house boy.
note: devastating revelation delivered matter-of-factly

quote: Get out of here and move forward. This never happened. It will shock you how much it never happened.
note: literal denial of reality

quote: What you call love was invented by guys like me to sell nylons.
note: cynical dismissal of love as concept

quote: I have a life and it only goes in one direction. Forward.
note: refuses to look back, denies past affects him

quote: I found out it was easier to be him than to start over.
note: cold detached statement of identity erasure

quote: You want some respect? Go out and get it for yourself.
note: dismisses need for connection

quote: I don't think about you at all.
note: ultimate denial of emotional engagement

quote: Bah! Humbug! What right have you to be merry? You are poor enough.
note: attacks the concept of positive emotion

quote: Every idiot who goes about with Merry Christmas on his lips should be boiled with his own pudding and buried with a stake of holly through his heart.
note: violent rejection of sentiment

quote: If I could work my will, every idiot who goes about with Merry Christmas on his lips should be boiled with his own pudding.
note: rage against human warmth

quote: What's Christmas time to you but a time for paying bills without money; a time for finding yourself a year older, but not an hour richer?
note: reduces human connection to economics

quote: I will be calm. I will be mistress of myself.
note: explicitly suppressing emotion to maintain control

quote: I do not attempt to deny that I think very highly of him, that I greatly esteem him, that I like him.
note: hedging/minimizing deep love with measured language

quote: I want no proof of their affection, but of their engagement I do.
note: redirecting from emotion to facts/evidence

quote: It is not fit that I should be judge of his conduct. I hardly know his intentions.
note: deflecting by claiming insufficient information

quote: My feelings shall be governed. I have been to blame in sinking under them.
note: treating feelings as weakness to be controlled

quote: I never cried for such a thing in my life: I hate going out in the carriage. I cry because I am miserable.
note: direct statement of emotional truth

quote: I am glad you are no relation of mine: I will never call you aunt again as long as I live. The very thought of you makes me sick, and that you treated me with miserable cruelty.
note: raw anger directly expressed

quote: Do you think, because I am poor, obscure, plain, and little, I am soulless and heartless? You think wrong! I have as much soul as you, and full as much heart!
note: passionate self-assertion of emotional depth

quote: I care for myself. The more solitary, the more friendless, the more unsustained I am, the more I will respect myself.
note: direct vulnerable statement of self-worth

quote: A lady's imagination is very rapid; it jumps from admiration to love, from love to matrimony, in a moment.
note: deflecting with observational wit instead of engaging emotionally

quote: My good opinion once lost, is lost forever.
note: rigid rule as shield against vulnerability of reconnection

quote: I was given good principles, but left to follow them in pride and conceit.
note: intellectual self-analysis instead of emotional engagement

quote: Disguise of every sort is my abhorrence.
note: asserting principle to avoid emotional topic

quote: I loved her against reason, against promise, against peace, against hope, against happiness, against all discouragement that could be.
note: raw unguarded admission of helpless love

quote: That was a memorable day to me, for it made great changes in me. But it is the same with any life.
note: reflective acknowledgment of being deeply affected

quote: In a word, I was too cowardly to do what I knew to be right, as I had been too cowardly to avoid doing what I knew to be wrong.
note: unflinching self-honesty about moral failure

quote: I have not broken your heart — you have broken it; and in breaking it, you have broken mine.
note: expresses pain but frames it as the other's fault, denying own vulnerability

quote: I seek no revenge on you. That's not the plan. The tyrant grinds down his slaves and they don't turn against him; they crush those beneath them.
note: cold philosophical framing of pain
